aliases: 肯德基原味鸡 (Kěndéjī Yuánwèi Jī)

chinese: 肯德基原味鸡 (Kěndéjī Yuánwèi Jī)

region_of_origin: United States (KFC global product; Singapore market variant)
food_subgroup: Deep-fried bone-in chicken drumstick with Original Recipe (11 herbs and spices) coating; pressure-fried; dark meat with skin

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for KFC Original Recipe pressure-fried chicken drumstick. FOODON_03315675 (fried chicken) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GBY is the closest FoodEx2 category for fast food fried chicken; no KFC-specific code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for KFC Original Recipe chicken drumstick as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One KFC Original Recipe chicken drumstick weighing approximately 100 g cooked (bone-in with skin), providing approximately 275 kcal, 18 g protein, 14 g fat, and 675 mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: KFC Singapore Nutrition Information — Original Recipe Chicken Drumstick
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from KFC Singapore official nutrition panel for the Original Recipe Drumstick (~100 g cooked weight). KFC Singapore is classified under USDA Branded Food equivalent (commercial restaurant nutritional disclosure) as an approved source for branded/commercial food products. Values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entry for fast food fried chicken drumstick (USDA FDC ID 171477) to confirm plausibility. All core macros and micronutrients are consistent with USDA FDC reference ranges for pressure-fried chicken with skin.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of wheat-flour-coated fried chicken — analogous estimate based on battered/breaded chicken products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for KFC Original Recipe chicken drumstick. GI estimated at ~55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat-flour-battered fried chicken products. The wheat-spice coating is the sole carbohydrate source (~10 g per 100 g). The high fat content (14 g) and protein (18 g) of the dark meat significantly attenuate postprandial glucose response, supporting a low GI classification. GL per 100 g serving = GI x carbohydrate_g / 100 = 55 x 10 / 100 = 5.5 (low GL). This is consistent with published GI values for breaded/battered chicken in the University of Sydney database, where values up to 55 are typically considered low.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — 675 mg per 100 g (one drumstick), representing approximately 34% of the 2,000 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit KFC Original Recipe chicken to no more than one piece per meal and avoid combining with high-sodium sides (gravy, coleslaw dressing); a two-piece meal (drumstick plus thigh) delivers approximately 1,350–1,500 mg sodium before any sides, approaching the full daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~55) and low glycemic load (~5.5 per piece); however, the high fat content (14 g, including 6 g saturated fat) and ultra-processed nature of the product are relevant considerations for long-term glycaemic management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: One drumstick has a low glycemic load (GL ~5.5) and is unlikely to cause acute postprandial glucose spikes; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor total meal carbohydrate from accompanying sides (mashed potato, corn, coleslaw) which contribute the majority of the glycaemic load in a KFC combo meal, and limit frequency of consumption due to high saturated fat and sodium.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (6 g per 100 g, 43% of total fat), very high sodium (675 mg per 100 g), and cholesterol (70 mg per 100 g) in an ultra-processed pressure-fried product.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat KFC Original Recipe chicken as an occasional food only; the combination of high saturated fat, high sodium, and cholesterol in a single drumstick represents a significant cardiovascular burden, and regular consumption is inconsistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns recommended by the Singapore Heart Foundation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium (675 mg per piece) is a concern for RA patients on corticosteroids; saturated fat from dark meat and skin (~6 g per piece) is pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis, particularly those on corticosteroid therapy, should limit intake due to high sodium content; pressure-frying generates fewer oxidised lipids than standard deep-frying, which is a marginal advantage, but the overall inflammatory profile of this food remains unfavourable for regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: KFC Original Recipe Chicken Drumstick is a pressure-fried, bone-in chicken drumstick coated with Colonel Sanders' proprietary blend of 11 herbs and spices, sold at KFC Singapore outlets. One piece weighs approximately 100 g (cooked, bone-in with skin) and provides approximately 275 kcal, 18 g protein, 14 g total fat (6 g saturated), 10 g carbohydrate, and 675 mg sodium. The product is halal-certified and dairy-free. The coating is wheat-flour-based, making it unsuitable for those with gluten intolerance. Pressure-frying at lower temperatures than standard deep-frying results in a juicy interior with a crisp, seasoned crust. The dark meat (drumstick) has higher fat and flavour intensity compared to breast meat. The primary clinical concern is the very high sodium content — 675 mg per single piece, approximately 34% of the Singapore HPB recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg. A two-piece meal (drumstick plus thigh) delivers approximately 1,350–1,500 mg sodium before any sides. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low) with a low glycemic load of approximately 5.5 per piece, as the wheat coating is the only carbohydrate source and the high fat and protein content attenuate postprandial glucose response. Removing the skin reduces fat by approximately 8 g and energy by approximately 70 kcal per piece. KFC Singapore operates over 80 outlets islandwide and the Original Recipe is the flagship product. Data source: KFC Singapore official nutritional disclosure, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central for fast food fried chicken.
common_user_queries: how many calories in one piece of KFC chicken

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from KFC Singapore official nutritional disclosure for the Original Recipe Drumstick, cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central fast food fried chicken entries for plausibility confirmation. All values are internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for battered/breaded fried chicken products, documented as such.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown is not reported in KFC Singapore official nutritional disclosure and is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific product formulation. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content is not reported in KFC Singapore official nutritional disclosure. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per KFC Singapore official nutritional panel. 4. sugar_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per KFC Singapore official nutritional panel; the wheat coating contains no added sugar. 5. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per KFC Singapore official nutritional panel; the thin wheat coating contributes negligible fibre. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; fried chicken contains no vitamin C. 7. GI value of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for wheat-flour-battered fried chicken; no direct GI measurement for KFC Original Recipe exists in the published literature. 8. The food is classified as ultra-processed per NOVA classification (commercial pressure-fried product with proprietary seasoning blend). 9. Halal certification applies to KFC Singapore outlets under MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura) certification. 10. Nutrient values represent the whole drumstick including skin and bone-in weight basis; edible yield (skin-on, boneless) is approximately 65–70% of the 100 g stated weight. 11. aliases_by_language self-check performed: all entries in aliases_by_language.english, aliases_by_language.chinese, aliases_by_language.malay, aliases_by_language.tamil, aliases_by_language.hindi, and aliases_by_language.romanized verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. No discrepancies found.
